title: tidymedia
text: R interface FFmpeg MediaInfo reproducible media preprocessing research/data-science pipelines — batch trimming, cropping, format standardization, metadata extraction tibbles. deliberately “ffmpeg R” (see cairn/DECISIONS.md D001).

title: tidymedia
text: Layer 0 — escape hatch: ffmpeg(), ffprobe(), mediainfo() pass raw arguments CLIs. “faithful wrapper” . Layer 1 — engine: ffm_* pipe builder (R/ffm.R, R/ffm_oop.R). command assembly, quoting, copy-vs-re-encode logic lives , . Deliberately linear: one input chain, sequential filters, one output, plus blessed multi-input verbs (stack/concat/overlay). filtergraph DAGs. Layer 2 — front door: task verbs (extract_audio(), segment_video(), …) thin wrappers Layer 1. Must glue command strings.

title: tidymedia
text: repo uses cairn plugin. project state lives markdown cairn/. Boundary rule: Architecture → DESIGN · Status → ROADMAP · Tasks → milestone files · Decisions → DECISIONS · History → archive + git. Start /milestone — status snapshot, health audit, suggested next action. Never record status TODOs file; anything time-varying rots . eight skills: /milestone, /milestone-plan, /milestone-implement, /milestone-review, /milestone-brief (Fable escalation), /hotfix, /cairn-release, /cairn-init. Work tiers: trivial edits (runtime surface) commit directly main; user-visible bugs go /hotfix; everything else milestone (/milestone-plan → /milestone-implement → /milestone-review). Ideas: “add X candidates” appends ROADMAP row — ceremony. Nothing merges main without user’s explicit approval review. Claude’s persistent memory never holds project state; cairn/ files win conflict. skills read plugin’s skills/shared/tracking-rules.md first obey .

title: Batch processing
text: ffm_batch() general-purpose engine: takes jobs tibble (one row per output) function turns row pipeline. column passed function name, purrr::pmap()-style, runner returns jobs tibble command column added (success column run = TRUE). build pipeline , combination builder verbs available per job. Give .f ... argument tolerates extra job columns use.
code: jobs <- tibble::tibble(   input  = c(video, video),   output = c("clip1.mp3", "clip2.mp3") )  ffm_batch(jobs, run = FALSE, .f = function(input, output, ...) {   ffm_files(input, output) |>     ffm_drop("video") |>     ffm_codec(audio = "libmp3lame") }) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   input                                                        output    command #>   <chr>                                                        <chr>     <chr>   #> 1 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4 clip1.mp3 "-y -i… #> 2 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4 clip2.mp3 "-y -i…

title: Batch processing
text: tasks turn one input many outputs. fan-verbs Layer 2 wrappers built ffm_batch(). segment_video() cuts file pieces given start/stop timestamps, returning one row per segment: separate_audio_video() splits file audio video streams, returning two compiled commands:
code: segment_video(   video,   start = c(0, 0.5),   end  = c(0.5, 1),   run = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   input                                               output start   end command #>   <chr>                                               <chr>  <dbl> <dbl> <chr>   #> 1 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/… /home…   0     0.5 "-y -i… #> 2 /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/… /home…   0.5   1   "-y -i… separate_audio_video(video, "audio.aac", "video.mp4", run = FALSE) #>                                                                                                         audio  #> "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -codec:a copy -map 0:a \"audio.aac\""  #>                                                                                                         video  #> "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -codec:v copy -map 0:v \"video.mp4\""

title: Batch processing
text: ffm_batch() fan-verbs accept parallel = TRUE, maps jobs furrr. Parallelism follows whatever future plan set, opt explicitly: Every job carries compiled command returned tibble, batch run fully reproducible: save column exact record FFmpeg commands produced outputs.
code: library(future) plan(multisession)  ffm_batch(jobs, parallel = TRUE, .f = function(input, output, ...) {   ffm_files(input, output) |> ffm_drop("video") |> ffm_codec(audio = "libmp3lame") })

title: Media metadata as tibbles
text: reader families differ backend (FFprobe vs. MediaInfo) return shape (tibble vs. single value). Pick need back: probe_*() family needs FFprobe installed; mediainfo_*() get_*() families need MediaInfo. probe_video() get_width() can report underlying fact (, frame width), choice usually comes shape want back tool installed.

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: tidymedia organized three layers, lowest highest level: Layer 0 — escape hatch. ffmpeg(), ffprobe(), mediainfo() pass raw argument string straight corresponding command-line tool. Use need something tidymedia wrap. Layer 1 — pipeline builder. ffm_* functions assemble FFmpeg command step step. quoting, option ordering, copy-vs-re-encode logic lives . Layer 2 — task verbs. Functions like extract_audio() segment_video() thin wrappers builder common jobs. See vignette("batch") processing many files . vignette focuses Layer 1, builder, heart package.

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: Every pipeline starts ffm() (alias ffm_files()), names input output files. Throughout vignette use tiny sample clip ships package: add steps |>. ffm_* verb records instruction; nothing runs ask . Calling ffm_compile() turns pipeline exact FFmpeg command represents — reproducible string can inspect, log, run: Printing pipeline shows compiled command, can eyeball pipeline point: ready, swap ffm_compile() ffm_run() execute command FFmpeg write output file.
code: video <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "tidymedia") ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_trim(start = 1, end = 5) |>   ffm_crop(width = 160, height = 120) |>   ffm_codec(video = "libx264") |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -vf \"trim=start=1:end=5,setpts=PTS-STARTPTS,crop=w=160:h=120:x=(in_w-out_w)/2:y=(in_h-out_h)/2\" -codec:v libx264 \"output.mp4\"" ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_scale(width = 320, height = 240) |>   ffm_pixel_format("yuv420p") #> tidymedia ffmpeg pipeline: #>  #>  -y -i "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4" -vf "scale=w=320:h=240" -pix_fmt yuv420p "output.mp4"

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: Cutting clip can frame-accurate (re-encoded, slower) fast lossless (stream copy snaps keyframes). ffm_seek() exposes via reencode argument, pairs naturally ffm_copy() fast path: Note ffm_seek() (uses FFmpeg’s -ss/-options) distinct ffm_trim() (trim filter): seeking can stream-copy.
code: # Fast, lossless cut ffm(video, "output.mp4") |>   ffm_seek(start = 1, end = 5, reencode = FALSE) |>   ffm_copy() |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -ss 1 -to 5 -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -codec:v copy -codec:a copy -avoid_negative_ts make_zero -map 0 \"output.mp4\""

title: Get started with tidymedia
text: “blessed” verbs take one input. Pass vector files ffm(), combine : stack side side ffm_hstack() top bottom ffm_vstack(), composite one another ffm_overlay(), join end--end ffm_concat(): manages stream labels internally, audio dropped unless map back ffm_map("0:"). task-verb layer wraps common cases: compare_videos() side--side (stacked) comparison picture_in_picture() inset overlay. builder stays deliberately linear — one input chain, sequential filters, one output — full FFmpeg filtergraphs scope. need one, reach Layer 0 escape hatch:
code: ffm(c(video, video), "side_by_side.mp4") |>   ffm_hstack() |>   ffm_compile() #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -filter_complex \"[0:v][1:v]hstack=inputs=2:shortest=0[vout]\" -map \"[vout]\" \"side_by_side.mp4\"" compare_videos(c(video, video), "compare.mp4", audio = 0, run = FALSE) #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -filter_complex \"[0:v][1:v]scale2ref='oh*mdar':'if(lt(main_h,ih),ih,main_h)'[0s][1s];[1s][0s]scale2ref='oh*mdar':'if(lt(main_h,ih),ih,main_h)'[1s][0s];[0s][1s]hstack,setsar=1[vout]\" -map \"[vout]\" -map 0:a \"compare.mp4\"" # Layer 0: raw arguments passed straight to FFmpeg ffmpeg("-version")[1] #> [1] "ffmpeg version 6.1.1-3ubuntu5 Copyright (c) 2000-2023 the FFmpeg developers"

title: Cover fixed regions of a video with opaque boxes — anonymize_video
text: infile string containing path video file. outfile string containing path video file write. regions data frame one row per box columns x, y, width, height (optionally color); see Details. color string naming default fill color FFmpeg color syntax, used row without color (default "black"). video_codec string naming output video codec (default "libx264"). pixel_format string naming output pixel format (default "yuv420p"). run logical: run command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) return compiled command without running (FALSE).

title: Cover fixed regions of a video with opaque boxes — anonymize_video
text: compiled FFmpeg command (invisibly run = TRUE).

title: Cover fixed regions of a video with opaque boxes — anonymize_video
text: regions data frame one row per box columns x, y, width, height (pixel number FFmpeg expression "in_w/2"); x/y give top-left corner width/height size. optional color column overrides color argument row. Every box solid fill (FFmpeg's drawbox t=fill); hollow outlines intentionally offered. filter applied, video re-encoded (video_codec / pixel_format, defaulting H.264 / yuv420p); odd source dimensions floored even output always encodes (yuv420p/libx264 requirement, -op already-even input). Audio stream-copied unchanged (-c:copy). input regions therefore always compile byte-identical command.

title: Cover fixed regions of a video with opaque boxes — anonymize_video
text: https://ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg-filters.html#drawbox

title: Cover fixed regions of a video with opaque boxes — anonymize_video
code: video <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "tidymedia") # Cover two fixed regions with black boxes regions <- data.frame(   x = c(10, 200), y = c(10, 150),   width = c(120, 80), height = c(90, 60) ) anonymize_video(video, "anon.mp4", regions, run = FALSE) #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -vf \"crop=w=floor(in_w/2)*2:h=floor(in_h/2)*2:x=(in_w-out_w)/2:y=(in_h-out_h)/2,drawbox=x=10:y=10:w=120:h=90:c=black:t=fill,drawbox=x=200:y=150:w=80:h=60:c=black:t=fill\" -codec:v libx264 -codec:a copy -pix_fmt yuv420p \"anon.mp4\""

title: Anonymize Many Videos From a Jobs Table — anonymize_video_batch
text: jobs data frame one row per input (least) input column (source path) regions list-column. regions cell data frame boxes input — x/y/width/height (optional per-box color) shape anonymize_video takes. optional output column names destination; absent, one derived per row appending _anonymized input's basename, keeping input's extension (e.g. clip.mkv becomes clip_anonymized.mkv). anonymization one-input--one-output, duplicated input output column collide rejected. three encode knobs — color, video_codec, pixel_format — may also appear column override corresponding argument per-row basis; rows (knobs) omit column fall back argument's value. columns ignored. color string naming default fill color (FFmpeg color syntax) applied every row, unless jobs carries color column box supplies color. (default = "black") video_codec string naming output video codec applied every row, unless jobs carries video_codec column. (default = "libx264") pixel_format string naming output pixel format applied every row, unless jobs carries pixel_format column. (default = "yuv420p") run logical: run input's command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) compile inspection (FALSE). parallel logical passed ffm_batch: anonymize parallel furrr (TRUE) sequentially (FALSE, default). Parallelism follows active future plan; TRUE default sequential plan runs one input time warns. Set plan first, e.g. future::plan(future::multisession). ... Additional arguments forwarded ffm_batch, verify, manifest, checksums, progress.

what: Run an FFmpeg Pipeline Over Many Files — ffm_batch
title: Run an FFmpeg Pipeline Over Many Files — ffm_batch
text: Apply pipeline-building function every row jobs table compile (optionally run) resulting FFmpeg command . tidymedia's batch-processing entry point: one reproducible compiled command per job, collected back tibble.

title: Run an FFmpeg Pipeline Over Many Files — ffm_batch
text: jobs data frame one row per job. column names arguments passed .f. .f function takes job's columns (name) returns ffm pipeline object. ... Additional arguments passed every call .f. run logical: run compiled command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) compile inspection (FALSE, dry run). parallel logical: map jobs parallel furrr (TRUE) sequentially (FALSE, default). Parallelism follows future plan caller set; TRUE default sequential plan, jobs still run one time warning issued. Set plan first, e.g. future::plan(future::multisession). verify optional output check applied job (run = TRUE). Either named list expected properties (spec every job, e.g. list(width = 1920)) function job columns (called pmap-style, like .f) returns list per job. job's output passed verify_media; unlike ffm_run, failed check recorded, aborted. Adds logical verified column (checks passed), NA jobs run successfully. progress logical: display cli progress bar jobs run (TRUE) run quietly (FALSE, default). applies run = TRUE; safe (-op animation) non-interactive sessions. manifest logical: TRUE (run = TRUE), record provenance manifest (per-job command, FFmpeg/FFprobe versions, timestamp, output size) attach result, readable ffm_manifest. (default = FALSE) checksums logical: TRUE, manifest also captures md5 checksums job's input(s) output. Ignored unless manifest = TRUE. (default = FALSE)

title: Run an FFmpeg Pipeline Over Many Files — ffm_batch
text: jobs tibble added command column (compiled FFmpeg command job) , run = TRUE, logical success column (plus verified column verify supplied). manifest = TRUE provenance manifest attached attribute; read ffm_manifest.

text: Append FFmpeg's loudnorm (EBU R128) audio filter, normalizing input's perceived loudness toward target integrated loudness, true-peak ceiling, loudness range. first builder function write pipeline's audio filter chain, compiles -af (joins existing audio filter chain application order).

title: Normalize Loudness in an FFmpeg Pipeline — ffm_loudnorm
code: ffm_loudnorm(   object,   target_loudness = -23,   true_peak = -1,   loudness_range = 7,   measured_i = NULL,   measured_tp = NULL,   measured_lra = NULL,   measured_thresh = NULL,   offset = NULL,   linear = FALSE,   print_format = NULL )

title: Normalize Loudness in an FFmpeg Pipeline — ffm_loudnorm
text: object ffmpeg pipeline (ffm) object created ffm_files(). target_loudness target integrated loudness, LUFS (number -70..-5; default -23, EBU R128 target). true_peak maximum true peak, dBTP (number -9..0; default -1, EBU R128 ceiling). loudness_range target loudness range, LU (number 1..50; default 7). measured_i, measured_tp, measured_lra, measured_thresh Measured input values prior loudnorm analysis pass (integrated loudness, true peak, loudness range, threshold). Supplied together drive accurate two-pass (linear) correction; five plus offset must given set, none (NULL, default, single-pass dynamic normalization). map FFmpeg's measured_I, measured_TP, measured_LRA, measured_thresh options. offset target_offset (offset gain) reported analysis pass, part measured set (see measured_i). NULL default. linear logical: TRUE, request linear normalization (linear=true), needs measured values hit target precisely. FALSE (default) omits option entirely, leaving single-pass dynamic behavior untouched. print_format measurement report format analysis pass, one "json", "summary", "none". NULL (default) omits option. Use "json" machine-parseable analysis pass.

what: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
text: Normalize perceived loudness file's audio toward EBU R128 target using FFmpeg's single-pass loudnorm filter, optionally downmixing channel count resampling. video stream copied unchanged (-c:v copy), audio touched – audio-side complement standardize_video, leaves audio alone.

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
code: normalize_audio(   infile,   outfile,   target_loudness = -23,   true_peak = -1,   loudness_range = 7,   channels = NULL,   sample_rate = NULL,   two_pass = FALSE,   run = TRUE )

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
text: infile string containing path media file (audio). outfile string containing path file write. target_loudness target integrated loudness, LUFS (number -70..-5; default -23, EBU R128 target). true_peak maximum true peak, dBTP (number -9..0; default -1, EBU R128 ceiling). loudness_range target loudness range, LU (number 1..50; default 7). channels output channel count, e.g. 1 downmix mono (positive whole number), NULL (default) keep source layout. sample_rate output sample rate Hz, e.g. 48000 (positive whole number), NULL (default) let loudnorm choose (resamples, 192 kHz encoder-capped – source rate). Set pin output rate. two_pass logical: TRUE, use accurate two-pass (measured/linear) normalization instead default single-pass (FALSE). first analysis pass measures input's loudness, second correction pass feeds measurements back linear=true output hits EBU R128 target precisely. Two-pass therefore always runs analysis pass FFmpeg (needs binary readable input), even run = FALSE: case analysis still runs returned value exact correction command, left unexecuted. single-pass default touches binary run = FALSE. input silent, analysis pass measures loudness -inf; normalizing silence target undefined, two-pass aborts clear error (single-pass default leaves silence untouched). run logical: run (correction) command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) return compiled command without running (FALSE). two_pass = TRUE gates correction pass; analysis pass runs regardless (see two_pass).

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
text: compiled FFmpeg command (invisibly run = TRUE). two_pass = TRUE correction command built measured values.

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
text: default targets follow EBU Recommendation R 128 (2014) – target_loudness = -23 LUFS true_peak = -1 dBTP, loudness measured per ITU-R BS.1770-4 – loudness_range = 7. single-pass (dynamic) loudnorm: input arguments always compile one reproducible command, separate measurement pass. audio filtered re-encoded (container's default audio encoder). Leaving channels NULL preserves source channel layout. Note FFmpeg's loudnorm filter resamples output (192 kHz, capped encoder), output sample rate source rate unless pin : set sample_rate control output rate.

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
text: EBU Recommendation R 128 (2014), Loudness normalisation permitted maximum level audio signals; ITU-R BS.1770-4.

title: Normalize a file's audio loudness (EBU R128) — normalize_audio
code: video <- system.file("extdata", "sample.mp4", package = "tidymedia") normalize_audio(video, "normalized.mp4", run = FALSE) #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -af \"loudnorm=I=-23:TP=-1:LRA=7\" -codec:v copy \"normalized.mp4\"" # Normalize to a streaming target and downmix to mono normalize_audio(video, "mono.mp4", target_loudness = -16, channels = 1,                 run = FALSE) #> [1] "-y -i \"/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/tidymedia/extdata/sample.mp4\" -af \"loudnorm=I=-16:TP=-1:LRA=7\" -codec:v copy -ac 1 \"mono.mp4\""

title: Normalize Many Files' Audio Loudness From a Jobs Table — normalize_audio_batch
text: Loudness-normalize audio many input files (EBU R128) single jobs tibble — batch (table-driven) sibling normalize_audio() one file normalize. row one input; required column names source. thin wrapper ffm_batch: one reproducible compiled command per input, sharing loudnorm pipeline (per-value validation) scalar verb. Set two_pass = TRUE accurate measured/linear normalization across whole table (see two_pass).

title: Normalize Many Files' Audio Loudness From a Jobs Table — normalize_audio_batch
code: normalize_audio_batch(   jobs,   target_loudness = -23,   true_peak = -1,   loudness_range = 7,   channels = NULL,   sample_rate = NULL,   two_pass = FALSE,   run = TRUE,   parallel = FALSE,   ... )

title: Normalize Many Files' Audio Loudness From a Jobs Table — normalize_audio_batch
text: jobs data frame one row per input (least) input column (source path). optional output column names destination; absent, one derived per row appending _normalized input's basename, keeping input's extension (e.g. clip.mkv becomes clip_normalized.mkv). normalization one-input--one-output, duplicated input output column collide rejected. five loudness knobs — target_loudness, true_peak, loudness_range, channels, sample_rate — may also appear column override corresponding argument per-row basis; rows (knobs) omit column fall back argument's value. columns ignored. target_loudness, true_peak, loudness_range EBU R128 loudness targets applied every row, unless jobs carries column name (see jobs). Defaults follow EBU Recommendation R 128 (2014): target_loudness = -23 LUFS, true_peak = -1 dBTP, loudness_range = 7 LU. channels output channel count applied every row, unless jobs carries channels column, e.g. 1 downmix mono. NULL (default) keeps source's channel layout. sample_rate output sample rate Hz applied every row, unless jobs carries sample_rate column. NULL (default) lets loudnorm choose (resamples, 192 kHz encoder-capped — source rate); set pin output rate. two_pass logical selecting batch normalization mode every row (two_pass whole-table switch, per-row column). FALSE (default) keeps single-pass loudnorm pipeline. TRUE runs accurate two-pass (measured/linear) path two-phase fan-: analysis pass first measures every input's loudness (honoring parallel row's targets), correction pass feeds measurements back linear=true output hits EBU R128 target precisely — table-wide sibling normalize_audio's two_pass. five measured values surfaced result columns measured_I, measured_TP, measured_LRA, measured_thresh, offset. must measure input, two-pass always runs analysis pass FFmpeg (needs binary readable inputs), even run = FALSE. row's analysis fails yields parseable measurement, call aborts — naming offending row(s) — correction command built. Silent rows exception: silent input (analysis loudness -inf) normalized target, one silent row abort batch — non-silent rows normalized, silent rows marked logical silent column (success = FALSE output written), warning names . single-pass default touches binary run = FALSE. run logical: run input's command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) compile inspection (FALSE). two_pass = TRUE gates correction pass; analysis pass runs regardless (see two_pass). parallel logical passed ffm_batch: normalize parallel furrr (TRUE) sequentially (FALSE, default). Parallelism follows active future plan; TRUE default sequential plan runs one input time warns. Set plan first, e.g. future::plan(future::multisession). ... Additional arguments forwarded ffm_batch, verify, manifest, checksums, progress.

title: Standardize a video to a reproducible format — standardize_video

Audio stream-copied unchanged (-c:copy); audio standardization scope. input therefore always compiles byte-identical command. Resolution follows width/height: supplying forces exact output dimensions; supplying one preserves aspect ratio rounds nearest even number (FFmpeg's -2); supplying neither keeps source resolution rounds odd dimensions nearest even value (yuv420p/libx264 requirement, -op already-even input) output always encodes.

text: jobs data frame one row per input (least) input column (source path). optional output column names destination; absent, one derived per row appending _stripped input's basename, keeping input's extension (e.g. clip.mkv becomes clip_stripped.mkv). two rows resolve output path — duplicated input output column, repeated explicit output — rejected one file silently overwrite another. columns ignored (scrub per-row knobs). run logical: run input's command FFmpeg (TRUE, default) compile inspection (FALSE). parallel logical passed ffm_batch: scrub parallel furrr (TRUE) sequentially (FALSE, default). Parallelism follows active future plan; TRUE default sequential plan runs one input time warns. Set plan first, e.g. future::plan(future::multisession). ... Additional arguments forwarded ffm_batch, verify, manifest, checksums, progress.

what: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
title: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
text: Probe media file check structural metadata set expectations, returning tidy pass/fail tibble one row per checked property. turns "command reproducible" "result asked ": use encode confirm output really duration, dimensions, codecs, pipeline meant produce.

title: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
code: verify_media(   file,   duration = NULL,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   video_codec = NULL,   audio_codec = NULL,   sample_rate = NULL,   ...,   tolerance = 0.1 )

title: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
text: file string naming single media file verify. duration Expected container duration seconds (numeric). width, height Expected video-frame dimensions pixels (numeric). video_codec, audio_codec Expected codec names first video audio stream (strings, e.g. "h264", "aac"). sample_rate Expected audio sample rate Hz (numeric). ... expectations given name = value, checked FFprobe field name (container first, video, audio). tolerance absolute tolerance numeric checks (default 0.1).

title: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
text: tibble one row per checked property columns file, check, expected, actual, pass (logical).

title: Verify a Media File Against Expected Properties — verify_media
text: Checks structural (drawn FFprobe metadata), perceptual: measure visual audio quality. named arguments cover common properties; pass FFprobe field name ... (example pix_fmt = "yuv420p" bit_rate = 141800). Extra names resolved probe columns order container, video stream, audio stream, first match wins. Numeric checks pass abs(actual - expected) <= tolerance; default tolerance 0.1 means integer properties (width, height, sample rate) must match exactly duration allowed little slack (e.g. keyframe-snapped cuts). String checks (codecs) must match exactly. property whose stream column absent yields NA actual value failing check.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: sample_frames() samples video fixed rate (fps) interval (interval, seconds frames) numbered image sequence — front door per-frame coding computer-vision feature pipelines. sample_frames_batch() across many videos jobs table.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: public API renamed single, predictable scheme. breaking changes deprecation shims (package still pre-1.0 soaking). Batch verbs now use _batch suffix instead plural noun: segment_videos() → segment_video_batch(), standardize_videos() → standardize_video_batch(), normalize_audios() → normalize_audio_batch(), anonymize_videos() → anonymize_video_batch(), extract_frames() → extract_frame_batch() (also removes confusion grabbing “many frames” one video). FFmpeg capability queries moved get_* namespace: get_codecs() → ffmpeg_codecs() get_encoders() → ffmpeg_encoders(). get_* now reserved per-file metadata getters. audio_as_mp3() now convert_audio(), new format argument. default (format = NULL) reproduces old behavior exactly (output format follows file extension); pass format pin audio codec. Metadata getters renamed match argument vocabulary: get_samplingrate() → get_sample_rate() get_framerate() → get_frame_rate(). Codec time-bound arguments harmonized: acodec/vcodec (matching jobs-table columns) now audio_codec/video_codec, segment_video()’s ts_start/ts_stop now start/end (matching batch columns). Removed unintended exports: unused tidy-eval reexports (enquo(), enquos(), as_label(), as_name(), :=) two internal helpers (pad_integers(), convert_fractions()) longer exported. .data remains reexported.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Help pages now cross-reference : every task verb links ffm_* pipeline builders built (builder back verbs use ), three metadata reader families (probe_*(), mediainfo_*(), get_*()) link one another can find alternative backend. metadata help page now states backend (FFprobe MediaInfo) returns (tibble, value, single scalar per file), “Media metadata tibbles” vignette gains table comparing reader families glance.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: New anonymize_video() covers one fixed rectangular regions video opaque filled boxes — redacting face, name badge, area stays one place whole clip (motion tracking). Regions given data frame x, y, width, height (numbers FFmpeg expressions), optional per-row color. video re-encoded reproducibly (H.264 / yuv420p default) audio stream-copied unchanged. New anonymize_videos() applies box-fill redaction across many videos one jobs tibble — row names input carries regions (list-column boxes data frames), optional per-row output, color, vcodec, pixel_format columns. Like table-driven verbs thin wrapper ffm_batch(), returning one reproducible command per input supporting verify, manifest, parallel execution.

what: Graceful handling of silent input in two-pass loudnorm
title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Two-pass normalization now handles digitally silent input honestly. Silence measures -inf loudness, normalized target. Previously surfaced misleading “parse loudnorm measurement” error. Now normalize_audio(two_pass = TRUE) aborts clear message names silence cause, normalize_audios(two_pass = TRUE) longer lets one silent row abort whole batch: non-silent rows normalized, silent rows marked new logical silent column (success = FALSE output written), warning names . Genuine analysis failures still abort fail-fast. (Near-silent non-empty audio unaffected.) two-pass batch’s result schema longer depends many rows silent: verify manifest requested, verified column provenance manifest now returned even every row silent (silent rows carry NA outputs), matching batch non-silent rows.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: normalize_audio() gained two_pass. two_pass = TRUE runs analysis pass measure input’s loudness, linear correction pass feeds measurements back, hitting EBU R128 target far precisely single-pass default material wide loudness range. must measure input, two-pass always calls FFmpeg — even run = FALSE, analysis still runs returned value exact correction command, left unexecuted. single-pass default unchanged stays binary-free run = FALSE. normalize_audios() gained two_pass , applying accurate measured/linear normalization across whole jobs table. two_pass = TRUE measures every input (honoring parallel row’s targets), builds runs one linear correction per row, surfacing five measured values measured_I/measured_TP/measured_LRA/ measured_thresh/offset columns. scalar verb analysis pass always runs — even run = FALSE, gates correction pass — row whose analysis yields usable measurement aborts call, naming offending row. two_pass whole-table switch, per-row column. single-pass default unchanged.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added normalize_audio(), task verb normalizes file’s perceived loudness EBU R128 target using FFmpeg’s single-pass loudnorm filter. default targets -23 LUFS integrated loudness -1 dBTP true-peak ceiling (EBU R128, measured per ITU-R BS.1770-4), copies video stream unchanged, preserves source channel layout, input always yields one reproducible command. Supply target_loudness, true_peak, loudness_range retarget, channels/sample_rate downmix resample audio. Note single-pass loudnorm resamples output (192 kHz, encoder-capped), set sample_rate pin output rate. Added ffm_loudnorm(), builder appends FFmpeg’s EBU R128 loudnorm audio filter pipeline — first builder write audio filter chain (-af).

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added normalize_audios(), table-driven companion normalize_audio(). Pass jobs tibble one row per input (input column required) loudness-normalize many files one call, EBU R128 target. returns tibble plus one reproducible command per row. five loudness knobs — target_loudness, true_peak, loudness_range, channels, sample_rate — may appear column vary per row, outputs auto-named <base>_normalized.<ext> output column given. thin wrapper ffm_batch(), ... forwards batch options verify, manifest, checksums, progress, parallel.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added standardize_video(), task verb re-encodes video reproducible, analysis-friendly format one call. default produces H.264 video yuv420p +faststart, stream-copies audio unchanged, keeps source resolution frame rate (rounding odd dimensions nearest even value codec can encode), input always yields byte-identical command. Supply width/height set output size (giving one preserves aspect ratio even output dimension), fps resample frame rate, vcodec/pixel_format override codec pixel format. Added ffm_fps(), builder appends fps filter pipeline, accepting either number frames per second FFmpeg framerate expression "30000/1001".

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added standardize_videos(), table-driven companion standardize_video(). Pass jobs tibble input column — one row per video — re-encode many files reproducible format one call. thin wrapper ffm_batch(), ... forwards batch options verify, manifest, checksums, progress, row compiles command byte-identical equivalent standardize_video() call. width, height, fps, vcodec, pixel_format may appear column override setting per row, otherwise function argument applies every row. output column optional: absent, names derived per input <basename>_standardized.<ext> (keeping source extension), duplicated input output column rejected rather silently overwritten.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added extract_frames(), table-driven companion extract_frame(). Pass jobs tibble input column exactly one timestamp frame column — one row per frame — grab still images spanning many input files one call. thin wrapper ffm_batch(), ... forwards batch options verify, manifest, checksums, progress. output column optional: absent, names derived per input file <basename>_<n>.<format> (default format = "png"), frame number restarting input.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: ffm_batch() (parallel = TRUE path segment_video() / segment_videos()) now warns parallel processing requested parallel future::plan() active. Previously calls ran one job time speedup indication; warning points future::plan(future::multisession).

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added segment_videos(), table-driven companion segment_video(). Pass jobs tibble input, output, start, end columns — one row per segment — cut segments spanning many input files one call. thin wrapper ffm_batch(), ... forwards batch options verify, manifest, checksums, progress; reencode selects accurate re-encoding (default) fast keyframe-snapping copy path, segment_video(). segment_videos() now reaches full parity segment_video(): output column optional (absent, names derived per input file <basename>_<n>.<ext>, numbering restarting input), per-row reencode column overrides scalar reencode argument, non-numeric/character start/end (non-logical reencode) columns rejected clear error instead opaque FFmpeg failure.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Added verify_media(), probe-backed checker confirms output really properties asked . returns tidy tibble one row per check (file, check, expected, actual, pass) covering duration, width, height, video_codec, audio_codec, sample_rate, plus FFprobe field passed name .... Numeric checks use absolute tolerance (default 0.1, integer dimensions match exactly duration gets little slack); codec checks match exactly. Verification wired execution. ffm_run(verify = <named list>) probes output successful run aborts, listing failed checks, assertion fails. ffm_batch(verify = <list function>) instead records outcome logical verified column (one spec jobs, pmap-style function job columns) without aborting. Added batch provenance manifest. ffm_batch(manifest = TRUE) attaches per-job record — command, FFmpeg/FFprobe versions, timestamp, output size — read back ffm_manifest(), can also write CSV via path =. checksums = TRUE additionally records input/output md5 checksums. ffm_batch(progress = TRUE) shows cli progress bar jobs run (following future plan parallel path).

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Completed blessed multi-input builder set ffm_vstack() (stack videos top bottom, vertical companion ffm_hstack()) ffm_overlay() (composite one video another x/y position given pixels FFmpeg expression). ffm_overlay() also takes optional scale resize overlay fraction main video’s width. Added two research task verbs built : compare_videos() side--side stacked comparison video, picture_in_picture() inset overlay (corner center position, scale, margin). drop audio default; pass audio = input index carry track.

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: Pipelines now executed argument vectors (via system2()), never shell string, input output paths containing spaces, quotes, $, backticks handled correctly. applies ffm_run(), ffm_batch(), every task verb; ffm_compile() still returns reproducible command string. Layer 0 escape hatches (ffmpeg(), ffprobe(), mediainfo()) keep raw-string interface. Raw output options added ffm_output_options() tokenized whitespace execution time; option values must contain spaces (never worked reliably ).

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: separate_audio_video() now stream-copies default — separation lossless fast, output container must support source codec. Use new reencode = TRUE argument previous re-encoding behavior. ffm_codec() ffm_pixel_format() now reject values single clean token (whitespace shell metacharacters, starting letter digit). ffm_run() — every task verb built — now aborts FFmpeg’s exit status encode fails, instead returning silently (old shell path emitted warning). ffm_batch() still records failures success column without aborting. ffm_output_options() now rejects option groups containing quote characters: options split whitespace arguments execution, quoting group tokens (previously commands executed different meaning printed).

title: tidymedia (development version)
text: explicit ffm_map() multi-input pipeline (e.g. ffm_hstack()) now emitted alongside automatic -map "[vout]" instead silently ignored, e.g. ffm_map(p, "0:") keeps first input’s audio next stacked video. Test coverage measured : empty R/zzz.R triggered covr bug silently reported 0% package coverage.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: First tagged release, bringing metadata, builder, task-verb work 0.0.0.900x development series documented, release-ready state.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: Every exported function now carries worked example architecture-layer @family tag, pkgdown site whose reference index grouped three layers (escape hatch, builder, task verbs). Added three vignettes: Get started (building pipelines, vignette("tidymedia")), Media metadata tibbles (vignette("metadata")), Batch processing (vignette("batch")). small sample clip now ships inst/extdata/sample.mp4 examples vignettes runnable.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: MediaInfo FFprobe readers now accept vector files return one stacked tibble keyed leading file column, metadata whole batch ready dplyr joins filters. covers probe_all(), probe_*() shortcuts, mediainfo_query(), mediainfo_template(), mediainfo_parameter(), get_*() convenience helpers. Typed output now default. Every reader gains typed argument (default TRUE) converts numeric columns integers/doubles turns missing markers (FFprobe’s "N/", MediaInfo’s empty values) NA; fractions, ratios, hex identifiers, text stay strings. Pass typed = FALSE previous -character behavior. replaces probe_all()’s convert argument. Readers resilient unreadable files: missing file, one probed, among several yields -NA row (NA value) plus warning, instead aborting whole call. Malformed arguments still abort. Arguments now passed CLIs argument vectors (system2()) rather interpolated shell string, file paths MediaInfo --Inform templates containing spaces, quotes, ;, %, $ work correctly. Layer 0 escape hatches mediainfo() / ffprobe() keep raw-string signatures. Output column schemas unified: readers lead file column two built-MediaInfo templates now emit snake_case column names. User-supplied names (mediainfo_query(names =), custom template headers) kept verbatim.

title: tidymedia 0.1.0
text: ffmpeg() longer lets FFmpeg read calling process’s standard input, running pipeline (e.g. via ffm_run()) inside script fed stdin longer swallows rest input. Equivalent FFmpeg’s -nostdin. probe_container(), probe_streams(), probe_video(), probe_audio() now return requested tibble called infile =; previously returned NULL. convert_fractions() parses fractions directly instead via eval(parse()), passes NA , errors values neither number fraction. FFprobe’s key=value output split first = , values contain = longer truncated; superseded tidyr::separate() call gone. Files zero streams longer trip stream loop.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Added ffm_batch(), tidymedia batch entry point: maps pipeline-building function every row jobs data frame (columns passed function name, purrr::pmap()-style), compiles one reproducible command per job, optionally runs . returns jobs tibble added command column , run, success column. Set parallel = TRUE map furrr following active future plan.

what: Task verbs rebuilt on the builder
title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Every task verb now thin wrapper Layer 1 ffm_* builder longer assembles FFmpeg command string. gains run argument returns compiled, reproducible command (invisibly run): extract_audio(), audio_as_mp3(), crop_video(), format_for_web(), extract_frame(), separate_audio_video(), segment_video(), concatenate_videos(). segment_video() now built ffm_batch(): returns job tibble (one row per segment command run status) gains reencode parallel arguments. Cutting frame-accurate default. segment_video() new ffm_seek() default reencode = TRUE, re-encodes cuts land exact requested frames. reencode = FALSE selects fast, lossless copy snaps nearest keyframes (output duration may differ one group--pictures). previous copy-based segment_video() cut wrong point shifted timestamps; behavior gone. Breaking: extract_audio()’s free-form options string replaced acodec argument; crop_video() drops arg argument now centers crop default; separate_audio_video() returns named vector two commands; segment_video() returns tibble.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9002
text: Added ffm_seek(), seek-based cut using -ss/-(distinct trim filter ffm_trim()), cuts can stream-copy. Accurate seeks output-seek re-encode; fast copy seeks input-seek add -avoid_negative_ts. Added ffm_concat(), blessed multi-input verb concatenates pipeline’s inputs via FFmpeg’s concat demuxer (fast, lossless, -format). Added ffm_output_options(), controlled passthrough raw output options ffm_compile() still positions quotes.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: Reworked Layer 1 ffm_* builder onto structured command model: ffm_compile() now single place assembles, positions, quotes every option. Single-input filter chains compile -vf/-af; multi-input stacking compiles valid -filter_complex graph explicit stream labels automatic -map. Fixed four builder bugs: ffm_trim(setpts = FALSE) longer forces setpts filter; ffm_drop() flags now output options placed input (-); ffm_pixel_format() longer runs output filename; previously invalid -filter_complex:v output gone. ffm_compile() now errors early stream set codec copy filter targets stream, instead failing cryptically ffmpeg. ffm_hstack() must applied video filters now produces runnable command (verified end--end ffmpeg).

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: Added testthat (3rd edition) test suite covering ffm_* pipeline builder ffm_compile() output, plus binary-gated tests ffmpeg/ffprobe/mediainfo task functions. Added GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check (macOS, Windows, Linux) test coverage; Linux jobs install ffmpeg mediainfo execution tests run CI. input validation user-facing messages now use rlang’s check_* helpers cli (cli::cli_abort() / cli::cli_warn()); assertthat dependency removed. Added dplyr, tidyr, purrr, cli Imports (first three already used undeclared). Enumerated arguments (e.g. units, unit, section, template, program) now matched exactly via rlang::arg_match() instead partial matching match.arg(); pass full value.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9001
text: mediainfo_parameter() (helpers built : get_duration(), get_framerate(), get_width(), get_height(), get_samplingrate()) now shell-quote --Inform argument, work POSIX shells ; previously parsed command separator.

title: tidymedia 0.0.0.9000
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
